words.def: a shape like a curve or circle made by a line curving right round
words.example: The road went in a huge loop around the lake.

words.def: the quality of being constant in your support of somebody/something
words.example: Can I count on your loyalty?

words.def: machines as a group, especially large ones
words.example: agricultural/industrial machinery

words.def: containing magic; used in magic
words.example: magical powers

words.def: an official who acts as a judge in the lowest courts of law
words.example: to come up before the magistrates

words.def: behaving like a magnet; that can be attracted by a magnet
words.example: magnetic materials

words.def: the great size or importance of something; the degree to which something is large or important
words.example: We did not realize the magnitude of the problem.

words.def: the main area of land of a country or region, not including any islands near to it
words.example: to/from the mainland, a boat to/from the mainland

words.def: considered normal because it reflects what is done or accepted by most people
words.example: mainstream culture/politics

words.def: the act of keeping something in good condition by checking or repairing it regularly
words.example: The school pays for heating and the maintenance of the buildings.

words.def: the authority to do something, given to a government or other organization by the people who vote for it in an election
words.example: It is undemocratic to govern an area without an electoral mandate.

words.def: required by law
words.example: The offence carries a mandatory life sentence.

words.def: to show something clearly, especially a feeling, an attitude or a quality
words.example: Social tensions were manifested in the recent political crisis.

words.def: to control or influence somebody/something, often in a dishonest way so that they do not realize it
words.example: manipulate somebody/something, She uses her charm to manipulate people.

words.def: behaviour that controls or influences somebody/something, often in a dishonest way so that they do not realize it
words.example: Advertising like this is a cynical manipulation of the elderly.

words.def: a copy of a book, piece of music, etc. before it has been printed
words.example: an unpublished/original manuscript

words.def: the 3rd month of the year, between February and April
words.example: She was born in March.

words.def: small and not important
words.example: a marginal improvement in weather conditions

words.def: connected with the sea and the creatures and plants that live there
words.example: marine life

words.def: the activity of competing with other companies to buy and sell goods, services, etc.
words.example: Companies must be able to survive in the marketplace.

words.def: something that you wear over your face to hide it, or to frighten or entertain other people
words.example: The robbers wore stocking masks.

words.def: the killing of a large number of people especially in a cruel way
words.example: the bloody massacre of innocent civilians

words.def: connected with or involving mathematics
words.example: mathematical calculations/problems/models

words.def: behaving in a sensible way, like an adult
words.example: Jane is very mature for her age.

words.def: to increase something as much as possible
words.example: to maximize efficiency/fitness/profits

words.def: serious and important
words.example: a meaningful relationship/discussion/experience

words.def: for a short period of time but not permanently
words.example: I'm changing my email address but for the meantime you can use the old one.

words.def: connected with the Middle Ages (about AD 1000 to AD 1450)
words.example: medieval architecture/castles/manuscripts

words.def: the practice of focusing your mind in silence, especially for religious reasons or in order to make your mind calm
words.example: She found peace through yoga and meditation.

words.def: a tune, especially the main tune in a piece of music written for several instruments or voices
words.example: a haunting melody

words.def: an official note from one person to another in the same organization
words.example: to write/send a memo

words.def: an account written by somebody, especially somebody famous, about their life and experiences
words.example: O'Connor published a childhood memoir.

words.def: a statue, stone, etc. that is built in order to remind people of an important past event or of a famous person who has died
words.example: a war memorial (= in memory of soldiers who died in a war)

words.def: an experienced person who advises and helps somebody with less experience over a period of time
words.example: She was a friend and mentor to many young actors.

words.def: a person who buys and sells goods in large quantities, especially one who imports and exports goods
words.example: a coal/wine merchant

words.def: a kind or forgiving attitude towards somebody that you have the power to harm or right to punish
words.example: to ask/beg/plead for mercy

words.def: used when you want to emphasize how small, unimportant, etc. somebody/something is
words.example: It took her a mere 20 minutes to win.

words.def: used meaning ‘only’ or ‘simply’ to emphasize a fact or something that you are saying
words.example: It is not merely a job, but a way of life.

words.def: to combine or make two or more things combine to form a single thing
words.example: The banks are set to merge next year.

words.def: the act of joining two or more organizations or businesses into one
words.example: If the merger goes through, thousands of jobs will be lost.

words.def: the quality of being good and of deserving praise or reward
words.example: a work of outstanding artistic merit

words.def: a set of methods and principles used to perform a particular activity
words.example: recent changes in the methodology of language teaching

words.def: the middle part of something
words.example: in the midst of something, Such beauty was unexpected in the midst of the city.

words.def: the movement every year of large numbers of birds or animals from one place to another
words.example: the seasonal migration of blue whales

words.def: using, or willing to use, force or strong pressure to achieve your aims, especially to achieve social or political change
words.example: militant groups/leaders

words.def: a group of people who are not professional soldiers but who have had military training and can act as an army
words.example: He said he would call out the state militia if the rebels did not surrender.

words.def: a building fitted with equipment for grinding grain into flour; a machine for grinding grain
words.example: The old mill has been converted into apartments.

words.def: very small in size or amount; as small as possible
words.example: The work was carried out at minimal cost.

words.def: to reduce something, especially something bad, to the lowest possible level
words.example: Good hygiene helps to minimize the risk of infection.

words.def: the process of getting coal and other minerals from under the ground; the industry involved in this
words.example: coal/diamond/gold/tin mining

words.def: a government department that has a particular area of responsibility
words.example: The Ministry of Defence has issued the following statement.

words.def: extremely small
words.example: minute amounts of chemicals in the water

words.def: an act or event that does not follow the laws of nature and is believed to be caused by God
words.example: the miracle of rising from the grave

words.def: great physical or mental pain
words.example: Fame brought her nothing but misery.

words.def: giving the wrong idea or impression and making you believe something that is not true
words.example: misleading information/advertisements

words.def: a weapon that is sent through the air and that explodes when it hits the thing that it is aimed at
words.example: nuclear missiles

words.def: a large crowd of people, especially one that may become violent or cause trouble
words.example: an angry/unruly mob

words.def: the ability to move easily from one place, social class or job to another
words.example: social/geographical/career mobility

words.def: to work together in order to achieve a particular aim; to organize a group of people to do this
words.example: The unions mobilized thousands of workers in a protest against the cuts.

words.def: that is neither very good, large, hot, etc. nor very bad, small, cold, etc.
words.example: students of moderate ability

words.def: the act or process of changing something in order to improve it or make it more acceptable; a change that is made
words.example: Considerable modification of the existing system is needed.

words.def: the ability to keep increasing or developing
words.example: The fight for his release gathers momentum each day.

words.def: a member of a religious group of men who often live apart from other people in a monastery and who do not marry or have personal possessions
words.example: Benedictine/Buddhist monks

words.def: the complete control of trade in particular goods or the supply of a particular service; a type of goods or a service that is controlled in this way
words.example: In the past central government had a monopoly on television broadcasting.

words.def: principles relating to right and wrong or good and bad behaviour
words.example: matters of public/private morality

words.def: a reason for doing something
words.example: There seemed to be no motive for the murder.

words.def: a person driving a car
words.example: The accident was reported by a passing motorist.

words.def: connected with or belonging to a town, city or district that has its own local government
words.example: municipal elections/councils

words.def: used to describe feelings that two or more people have for each other equally, or actions that affect two or more people equally
words.example: mutual respect/understanding

words.def: used to introduce more exact and detailed information about something that you have just mentioned
words.example: We need to concentrate on our target audience, namely women aged between 20 and 30.

words.def: happening or existing in all parts of a particular country
words.example: a nationwide campaign

words.def: connected with the navy of a country
words.example: a naval base/officer/battle

words.def: the fact of not giving enough care or attention to something/somebody; the state of not receiving enough care or attention
words.example: The buildings are crumbling from years of neglect.

words.def: located or living near or next to a place or person
words.example: a neighbouring house

words.def: a hollow place or structure that a bird makes or chooses for laying its eggs in and sheltering its young
words.example: sparrows building a nest of twigs and dry grass

words.def: a net amount of money is the amount that remains when nothing more is to be taken away
words.example: a net profit of £500

words.def: a report containing news of the activities of a club or organization that is sent regularly to all its members
words.example: Our sailing club produces a monthly newsletter.

words.def: a comfortable or suitable role, job, way of life, etc.
words.example: He eventually found his niche in sports journalism.

words.def: having or showing fine personal qualities that people admire, such as courage, honesty and care for others
words.example: a noble leader

words.def: if you nod, nod your head or your head nods, you move your head up and down to show agreement, understanding, etc.
words.example: I asked him if he would help me and he nodded.

words.def: to formally suggest that somebody/something should be chosen for an important role, prize, position, etc.
words.example: nominate somebody/something (for something), She has been nominated for the presidency.

words.def: the act of suggesting or choosing somebody as a candidate in an election, or for a job or an award; the fact of being suggested for this
words.example: Membership of the club is by nomination only.

words.def: a person who has been formally suggested for a job, a prize, etc.
words.example: He was chosen as the party's presidential nominee.

words.def: without the aim of making a profit
words.example: an independent non-profit organization

words.def: despite this fact
words.example: The book is too long but, nonetheless, informative and entertaining.

words.def: ideas, statements or beliefs that you think are silly or not true
words.example: Reports that he has resigned are nonsense.

words.def: 12 o’clock in the middle of the day
words.example: We should be there by noon.

words.def: deserving to be noticed or to receive attention; important
words.example: a notable success/achievement/example

words.def: used for giving a good or the most important example of something
words.example: The house had many drawbacks, most notably its price.

words.def: to formally or officially tell somebody about something
words.example: notify somebody, Competition winners will be notified by post.

words.def: well known for being bad
words.example: a notorious criminal

words.def: different from anything known before; new, interesting and often seeming slightly strange
words.example: a novel feature

words.def: a place where young children are cared for while their parents are at work; a day nursery or nursery school
words.example: at nursery, Her youngest child is at nursery now.

words.def: a reason why you do not like or are opposed to something; a statement about this
words.example: I'd like to come too, if you have no objection.

words.def: to force somebody to do something, by law, because it is a duty, etc.
words.example: Parents are obliged by law to send their children to school.

words.def: to completely fill your mind so that you cannot think of anything else, in a way that is not reasonable or normal
words.example: be obsessed by somebody/something, He's obsessed by computers.

words.def: the state in which a person’s mind is completely filled with thoughts of one particular thing or person in a way that is not reasonable or normal
words.example: Her fear of flying is bordering on obsession.

words.def: happening or done sometimes but not often
words.example: He works for us on an occasional basis.

words.def: something that happens or exists
words.example: a common/everyday/frequent/regular occurrence

words.def: the degree to which something is likely to happen
words.example: The odds are very much in our favour (= we are likely to succeed).

words.def: something that is produced for other people to use, watch, enjoy, etc.
words.example: the latest offering from the Canadian-born writer

words.def: a child of a particular person or couple
words.example: the problems parents have with their teenage offspring

words.def: connected with the way in which a business, machine, system, etc. works
words.example: operational activities/costs/difficulties

words.def: to choose to take or not to take a particular course of action
words.example: opt for/against something, After graduating she opted for a career in music.

words.def: connected with the sense of sight or the relationship between light and sight
words.example: optical effects

words.def: a feeling that good things will happen and that something will be successful
words.example: a mood of cautious optimism

words.def: spoken rather than written
words.example: a test of both oral and written French

words.def: connected with the way in which the different parts of something are arranged; connected with an organization
words.example: organizational changes within the party

words.def: a person’s basic beliefs or feelings about a particular subject
words.example: religious/political orientation

words.def: to happen or appear for the first time in a particular place or situation
words.example: The disease is thought to have originated in the tropics.

words.def: the sudden start of something unpleasant, especially violence or a disease
words.example: the outbreak of war

words.def: a trip that you go on for pleasure or education, usually with a group of people and lasting no more than one day
words.example: a family outing

words.def: a shop or an organization that sells goods made by a particular company or of a particular type
words.example: The business has 34 retail outlets in this state alone.

words.def: the attitude to life and the world of a particular person, group or culture
words.example: Travel broadens your outlook.

words.def: a strong feeling of shock and anger
words.example: The judge's remarks caused public outrage.

words.def: a person who is not accepted as a member of a society, group, etc.
words.example: Here she felt she would always be an outsider.

words.def: to fail to see or notice something
words.example: He seems to have overlooked one important fact.

words.def: too; very
words.example: I'm not overly fond of pasta.

words.def: to watch somebody/something and make sure that a job or an activity is done correctly
words.example: United Nations observers oversaw the elections.

words.def: if something overturns, or if somebody overturns it, it turns into a position in which the top of it is where the bottom of it normally is or on its side
words.example: The car skidded and overturned.
